info.name: Splunk Enterprise REST API
info.description:
The Splunk Enterprise REST API provides programmatic access to Splunk Enterprise functionality including search, indexing, data inputs, and system management. All requests are made over HTTPS to the splunkd management port (default 8089). Authentication is required via Splunk authentication tokens or HTTP Basic authentication with a valid Splunk username and password. Responses are available in JSON and XML formats.

Contact Support:
 Name: Splunk Inc.
 Email: devinfo@splunk.com

Time to live in seconds (used with setttl action)
Priority value 0-10 (used with setpriority action)

request.description: Runs a search and streams results back as they become available, rather than waiting for the search to complete. This is a streaming endpoint suitable for long-running searches or real-time searches. The search runs synchronously and results stream back in the response.

response.name: Search results streaming successfully

urlencoded.description: (Required) The SPL search query to execute
Earliest time for the search using relative or absolute time format
Latest time for the search
The search mode
Execution mode. Normal runs asynchronously, oneshot runs synchronously, blocking waits for completion.
Maximum number of results to return
Maximum time in seconds before the search is finalized
Number of seconds to keep the search after processing has stopped
Comma-separated list of required fields to include in results
Application namespace for the search
Optional custom search ID. If not specified, Splunk generates one automatically.
Number of status buckets to generate for the search timeline. Set to a value greater than 0 to enable timeline.
Seconds of inactivity after which the search is automatically cancelled. 0 means never auto-cancel.
Auto-finalize the search after this number of events have been processed. 0 disables auto-finalize.
Seconds of inactivity after which the search is automatically paused. 0 means never auto-pause.
Whether to enable lookups during the search
Whether to reload macro definitions before the search
How frequently to invoke the reduce phase (seconds)
Whether to run the search in a separate process

request.description: Creates a new search job. The search job runs asynchronously. Use the returned search ID (sid) to check job status and retrieve results. Searches use the Splunk Search Processing Language (SPL).

response.name: Search job created successfully

item.name: Send Events via Http Event Collector

request.description: Sends one or more events to Splunk via HTTP Event Collector. Events are submitted as JSON objects. Multiple events can be sent in a single request by concatenating JSON objects.
